messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

{"svg":"<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"512\" height=\"512\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">\
  <defs>\
    <radialGradient id=\"bg\" cx=\"50%\" cy=\"30%\" r=\"70%\">\
      <stop offset=\"0%\" stop-color=\"#fff8f0\"/>\
      <stop offset=\"100%\" stop-color=\"#ffe7d9\"/>\
    </radialGradient>\
    <linearGradient id=\"glow\" x1=\"0\" x2=\"0\" y1=\"0\" y2=\"1\">\
      <stop offset=\"0%\" stop-color=\"#ffffff\" stop-opacity=\"0.35\"/>\
      <stop offset=\"100%\" stop-color=\"#ffffff\" stop-opacity=\"0\"/>\
    </linearGradient>\
  </defs>\
  <rect width=\"512\" height=\"512\" fill=\"url(#bg)\"/>\
  <!-- mother hair -->\
  <ellipse cx=\"220\" cy=\"140\" rx=\"70\" ry=\"54\" fill=\"#5b3a2e\"/>\
  <!-- mother head -->\
  <circle cx=\"220\" cy=\"150\" r=\"44\" fill=\"#f6c0a0\"/>\
  <!-- mother body (dress) -->\
  <path d=\"M160 320 L220 190 L280 320 Z\" fill=\"#f7a8c2\"/>\
  <!-- child body and head -->\
  <ellipse cx=\"280\" cy=\"270\" rx=\"44\" ry=\"34\" fill=\"#7fd3e6\"/>\
  <circle cx=\"280\" cy=\"210\" r=\"28\" fill=\"#f6c0a0\"/>\
  <!-- arms hugging (in front) -->\
  <path d=\"M195 210 C180 240 220 280 260 275\" stroke=\"#f6c0a0\" stroke-width=\"26\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" fill=\"none\"/>\
  <path d=\"M245 230 C270 250 300 255 315 230\" stroke=\"#f6c0a0\" stroke-width=\"20\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" fill=\"none\"/>\
  <!-- small heart -->\
  <path d=\"M240 245 C230 220 200 220 202 250 C204 280 240 300 240 300 C240 300 276 280 278 250 C280 220 250 220 240 245 Z\" fill=\"#ff6b6b\"/>\
  <!-- subtle highlight -->\
  <ellipse cx=\"220\" cy=\"140\" rx=\"48\" ry=\"30\" fill=\"url(#glow)\" opacity=\"0.6\"/>\
</svg>"}
message.reasoning:
**Creating an SVG design**

I’m thinking of making an SVG with a soft gradient background and a circle halo. It will have two simplified figures: a mother with an oval head and a triangular body, embracing a child with a smaller circle head. I want to add a heart shape between them, with no labels. The layout will be centered, and I plan to use a radial gradient from light peach to white for the background. I’ll keep the shapes simple.**Crafting the SVG string**

I'll start by designing the SVG coordinates. The background will be a rectangle with a radial gradient. For the mother, I'll have a big curved path for the hair, with her head being a circle slightly left of center. I want her to hold the child close, so I’ll create a trapezoid for her body and arms that wrap around the child. The child's body will be a small rounded rectangle or oval. A small red heart will be placed between them, and I'll make sure to escape quotes in the JSON string properly.**Writing the SVG string**

I'll create a JSON object with the SVG string included. First, I’ll set up the SVG tag with a viewBox and preserveAspectRatio. For the background, I'll define a radial gradient and then create a rectangle for the background color.  

Next, I'll add shapes for the mother figure. I need a path for the hair, which I’ll adjust to be simpler as an ellipse behind her head. The head will be a circle that’s centered on top. Finally, I’ll make the body a triangular path representing her dress.**Designing the child's figure**

For the child, I’ll create a head circle positioned at cx=280, cy=210, with a radius of 28 in the same skin color. The body will be an ellipse at cx=280, cy=260, choosing between a light skin color or teal for clothes.  

Next, I need to craft the mother’s left arm as a path that thickly strokes in skin color, curving around the child's back. I'll carefully consider the layering: to show the hugging action, I want the arms to appear behind the child initially, and then adjust the drawing order to have the arms in front. Lastly, a heart shape will be placed at the center between them.{"svg":"<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">

message.reasoning:
**Preparing SVG for JSON**

I need to embed an SVG string within a JSON string, so I have to make sure to escape double quotes properly. First, I’ll craft the SVG using double quotes, remembering that when I insert this into the JSON value, I need to escape those quotes with backslashes. It’s important to focus on getting this right because the assistant should output only JSON. I’ll start by building the SVG first and then proceed with escaping it.**Crafting SVG Content**

I'm working with SVG content that includes various shapes and structures like rectangles, ellipses, paths, and circles to depict a scene. I need to ensure that all components like hair, heads, bodies, and even hearts are correctly formatted in the SVG. Each element has designated attributes like color and dimensions. I must pay attention to details, especially for the stroke outlines and fill colors. It’s essential to construct this SVG correctly so it can be effectively embedded and utilized in a JSON format later on.**Finalizing SVG to JSON**

I’m wrapping up my SVG by ensuring there’s no text or visible letters/numbers in the drawing, which aligns with the guidelines. I need to produce a JSON string formatted like: {"svg":"<svg...>...</svg>"}, where I escape the inner quotes properly. Although the path data uses letters like 'M' or 'C', that’s allowed since they’re not considered visible text. I’ll make sure to use \n for newlines in the JSON to be safe. Now, I can create the final JSON string!**Generating JSON with SVG**

I noticed that I can embed actual newlines in a JSON string, but it's generally safer to escape them as \n. So, I’ll embed these newlines using the \n sequence to be cautious. My plan is to create a single string with double quotes escaped as \" and to ensure there are no unnecessary backslashes apart from those for escapes. I'll focus on maintaining minimal whitespace for cleanliness, even if readability isn’t the primary concern. Now, I'll generate the final JSON!

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#FFF7F2"/>

  <!-- Heart above -->
  <path d="M256 100 C236 60 186 60 186 110 C186 150 236 180 256 200 C276 180 326 150 326 110 C326 60 276 60 256 100 Z" fill="#FF4D6D" />

  <!-- Child (right) body -->
  <path d="M290 240 Q320 320 350 240 L350 340 Q320 370 290 340 Z" fill="#7FC8FF" />
  <!-- Child head -->
  <circle cx="320" cy="180" r="34" fill="#F1C27D" />
  <!-- Child hair -->
  <path d="M292 170 Q320 140 348 170 Q320 160 292 170 Z" fill="#6B3F2A" />

  <!-- Adult (left) body -->
  <path d="M120 240 Q180 360 240 240 L240 380 Q180 420 120 380 Z" fill="#FF93B3" />
  <!-- Adult head -->
  <circle cx="180" cy="150" r="50" fill="#F1C27D" />
  <!-- Adult hair -->
  <path d="M130 150 Q180 90 230 150 Q180 130 130 150 Z" fill="#6B3F2A" />

  <!-- Arms hugging (drawn as thick strokes) -->
  <path d="M220 220 Q260 250 280 290" stroke="#F1C27D" stroke-width="28" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none" />
  <path d="M140 220 Q180 250 210 260" stroke="#F1C27D" stroke-width="28" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none" />

  <!-- Overlap: small highlights for warmth -->
  <ellipse cx="200" cy="320" rx="40" ry="10" fill="#FF7EA0" opacity="0.12" />
  <ellipse cx="320" cy="320" rx="30" ry="8" fill="#66B8FF" opacity="0.12" />
</svg>"

message.reasoning:
**Designing an SVG for “maman”**

I’m working on creating an SVG representation of the word "maman" without any text. The idea is to symbolize the concept of a mother through simple silhouettes, depicting a larger adult figure and a smaller child, maybe holding hands or hugging. I want to use gentle colors and rounded shapes to create a warm, inviting feel. The adult figure will be on the left, the child on the right, with a small heart above them, all within a soft pastel background.**Creating SVG Elements**

I'm focusing on creating an SVG design for the mother and child representation. The adult's head will be a circle with specified coordinates and fill color, while the child's head will be smaller. Instead of detailed body designs, I’ll simplify things by using trapezoids for dresses, with a pink dress for the adult and blue for the child. I’ll also include arms curving around the child, a heart above them, and ensure the final output is valid JSON containing the SVG string with properly escaped quotes.**Building SVG Elements**

I’m creating SVG content for the mother and child representation. First, I’ll set the overall dimensions and background color. For the adult figure, I’ll use an ellipse for the body and a circle for the head, both with carefully chosen colors. I want to add arms that simulate a hugging gesture, using curved paths that match the dress color. The child will also have a smaller body and head. Lastly, I’ll include a heart shape at the top center and ensure that there’s no stray text in the design.**Deciding on Features for SVG**

I'm considering how to represent facial features in the SVG design. I think using simple shapes like circles for eyes could work, but I want to stick to the rule of no letters or numbers. Small dots might be acceptable for the eyes, but I guess it would be best to keep things minimal without detailed features to maintain simplicity. I want the overall design to focus on the mother and child concept without complicating it too much.**Assembling Coordinates for SVG**

I'm compiling the coordinates and shapes for the SVG design. The adult's head will be a circle, and I'll use a path for hair that covers the head with a quadratic curve for a nice shape. The body will be another path representing a dress. The child's head will also be a circle with the same skin tone, and I’ll create a simple bob for the hair as a path. 

I'll design the child’s body similarly to the adult's dress but in a different color. The arms should hug the child, with paths to indicate their curves. I also want to add a simple heart shape above. Finally, I’ll ensure everything is in the correct layering and set the SVG string in a valid JSON format while maintaining correct escape sequences.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:

You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.

{"svg":"<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"512\" height=\"512\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">\
  <rect width=\"512\" height=\"512\" fill=\"#FFF8F0\"/>\
  <!-- mother's clothing -->\
  <ellipse cx=\"256\" cy=\"330\" rx=\"130\" ry=\"150\" fill=\"#FF9FA3\"/>\
  <!-- child's clothing -->\
  <ellipse cx=\"256\" cy=\"360\" rx=\"70\" ry=\"90\" fill=\"#FFD6A5\"/>\
  <!-- mother's hair behind head -->\
  <ellipse cx=\"256\" cy=\"135\" rx=\"70\" ry=\"50\" fill=\"#5D4037\"/>\
  <!-- mother's head -->\
  <circle cx=\"256\" cy=\"150\" r=\"60\" fill=\"#F6C9B6\"/>\
  <!-- child's hair and head -->\
  <ellipse cx=\"256\" cy=\"210\" rx=\"42\" ry=\"28\" fill=\"#5D4037\"/>\
  <circle cx=\"256\" cy=\"220\" r=\"36\" fill=\"#F6C9B6\"/>\
  <!-- hugging arms (skin tone strokes) -->\
  <path d=\"M160 260 C140 300 180 380 240 380\" stroke=\"#F6C9B6\" stroke-width=\"34\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" fill=\"none\"/>\
  <path d=\"M352 260 C372 300 330 380 272 380\" stroke=\"#F6C9B6\" stroke-width=\"34\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" fill=\"none\"/>\
  <!-- faces: eyes and smiles -->\
  <circle cx=\"238\" cy=\"150\" r=\"4\" fill=\"#3E2723\"/>\
  <circle cx=\"274\" cy=\"150\" r=\"4\" fill=\"#3E2723\"/>\
  <path d=\"M236 170 Q256 185 276 170\" stroke=\"#3E2723\" stroke-width=\"3\" fill=\"none\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\"/>\
  <circle cx=\"246\" cy=\"220\" r=\"3\" fill=\"#3E2723\"/>\
  <circle cx=\"266\" cy=\"220\" r=\"3\" fill=\"#3E2723\"/>\
  <path d=\"M246 235 Q256 245 266 235\" stroke=\"#3E2723\" stroke-width=\"2.5\" fill=\"none\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\"/>\
  <!-- small heart above to suggest love/motherhood -->\
  <path d=\"M256 90 C246 70 206 76 206 106 C206 136 236 156 256 176 C276 156 306 136 306 106 C306 76 266 70 256 90 Z\" fill=\"#FF6B6B\"/>\
</svg>"}
message.reasoning:
**Creating a drawing for "maman"**

I'm working on a drawing for the one-word guessing game with the target word "maman," which means "mom" in French. I need to represent a mother figure visually using SVG shapes only, without any text. 

The idea is to depict a larger mother figure embracing a smaller child figure. I'll use circle shapes for heads, with the mother's body being a rounded rectangle or ellipse. I might include some soft colors and floral elements, but I want to keep it simple and ensure no letters or numbers are included.**Designing a simple SVG for "maman"**

I'm creating a simple SVG with a viewBox of 0 0 512 by 512 pixels, avoiding scripts or text. I want a pastel background and will draw a mother’s hair as a brown semicircle. I'll use circles for heads and rounded rectangles for bodies, with ellipses for shoulders. The arms will be hugging the child, represented by a small heart above them for affection. I'll ensure the mother's figure is larger and design simple dot eyes and arc smiles for faces. The final drawing will fit the canvas perfectly!**Placing mother figure in the design**

I'm centering the mother figure in the SVG design. The background will be a light cream rectangle. For the mother’s body, I’ll use a large ellipse at (256, 340) with a skin or clothing color, maybe coral. I'll use a light skin tone for the mother's head and dark brown for her hair. The child’s head will be a smaller circle, positioned lower at (256, 220). I’ll have the mother’s arms hugging around the child, with matching clothing colors for a cohesive look. Finally, I’ll create a smaller body ellipse for the child in front.**Adding details for the mother figure**

I’m adding hair for the mother, along with simple facial features like small circles for eyes and smiles made of paths. I plan to include a red heart above them to convey affection while ensuring there are no text elements in the SVG.

Now, I’ll craft the SVG string carefully, remembering to escape quotes in JSON to keep everything neat and valid. I’ll construct the shapes, starting with a background rectangle, clothing ellipses for both the mother and child, and circles for their heads. The hair will be a path over the mother’s head.**Creating final JSON output for SVG**

I'm finalizing the SVG representation with elements like the mother and child figures, their facial features, and the heart above them. I'll ensure the SVG markup is structured correctly and all quotes are properly escaped for JSON. 

It’s essential to remember that using numeric coordinates is acceptable; we just need to avoid including any text or labels visually. After crafting the SVG content neatly, I’ll wrap it in the JSON format with the field "svg". Here it goes:

{"svg":"<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"512\" height=\"512\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">\
  <defs>\
    <linearGradient id=\"bg\" x1=\"0\" y1=\"0\" x2=\"1\" y2=\"1\">\
      <stop offset=\"0\" stop-color=\"#FFF7FB\"/>\
      <stop offset=\"1\" stop-color=\"#FFEFF3\"/>\
    </linearGradient>\
  </defs>\
  <rect width=\"512\" height=\"512\" fill=\"url(#bg)\"/>\
  <ellipse cx=\"256\" cy=\"480\" rx=\"160\" ry=\"28\" fill=\"#FFE6E9\" opacity=\"0.6\"/>\
  <!-- mother silhouette -->\
  <circle cx=\"190\" cy=\"150\" r=\"48\" fill=\"#FAD6C4\"/>\
  <path d=\"M150 200 C120 260 120 360 200 400 C280 360 280 260 240 200 Z\" fill=\"#FF8DAA\"/>\
  <!-- child silhouette -->\
  <circle cx=\"300\" cy=\"220\" r=\"28\" fill=\"#FAD6C4\"/>\
  <path d=\"M270 250 C250 290 250 330 290 360 C330 330 330 290 310 250 Z\" fill=\"#FFD1A6\"/>\
  <!-- mother's arm hugging child -->\
  <path d=\"M220 240 C240 260 260 270 280 260\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#FF8DAA\" stroke-width=\"20\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" opacity=\"0.95\"/>\
  <circle cx=\"283\" cy=\"260\" r=\"10\" fill=\"#FAD6C4\"/>\
  <!-- heart above to suggest 'mom' affection -->\
  <path d=\"M260 110 C246 86 212 92 212 116 C212 140 240 152 260 176 C280 152 308 140 308 116 C308 92 274 86 260 110 Z\" fill=\"#FF6B81\"/>\
</svg>"}
